summary: The first paper explores using visual cooperative drone tracking to support open-path gas measurements, combining Tunable Diode Laser Absorption Spectroscopy with gas tomography to map gas concentrations like CO2 or methane. The second paper introduces a multi-physics simulation framework for designing and evaluating high-power microwave counter-UAS systems, focusing on non-kinetic neutralization methods that resist radio-frequency jamming. The third paper presents Diamond, an authenticated encryption framework tailored for resource-constrained IoT devices such as small drones, ensuring secure telemetry transmission under strict computing and energy limits. The fourth paper proposes YolovN-CBi, a lightweight neural network architecture optimized for real-time detection of small UAVs, addressing challenges posed by their size, speed, and low visual contrast. The fifth paper introduces SDG-Track, a heterogeneous observer-follower framework for high-resolution UAV tracking on embedded platforms, tackling the resolution-speed trade-off by avoiding downsampling that degrades small target detection.
